799	For a commutative ring `R`, an ideal `I ⊆ R`, and two `R`-modules `M, M'` with
800	an `R`-linear equivalence `e : M ≃ₗ[R] M'`, we have `depth I M = depth I M'`.
801	
802	This is the standard "depth is an invariant of the isomorphism class" fact;
803	the proof has two steps: (1) the side-condition `I • ⊤ = ⊤` is preserved
804	under linear equivalence, and (2) the regular-sequence supremum sets agree
805	via `LinearEquiv.isRegular_congr`. Both halves are direct Mathlib calls; the
806	helper is axiom-clean.
807	
808	iter-193 Lane G structural advance: this helper is the substrate piece
809	needed to identify `depth(M)` with `depth(R^k)` for `M` finite free, which
810	in turn closes the `pd(M) = 0` base case of the Auslander–Buchsbaum
811	formula modulo the `depth(R^k) = depth(R)` direct-sum step (a separate
812	substrate item; the file's residual sorries are narrowed to that one
813	named piece). -/
814	lemma depth_eq_of_linearEquiv {R : Type u} [CommRing R] (I : Ideal R)
815	    {M M' : Type v} [AddCommGroup M] [Module R M] [AddCommGroup M'] [Module R M']
816	    (e : M ≃ₗ[R] M') :
817	    depth I M = depth I M' := by
818	  -- Step 1: `I • ⊤ = ⊤` is preserved under the linear equivalence.
819	  have hcond : (I • (⊤ : Submodule R M) = ⊤) ↔ (I • (⊤ : Submodule R M') = ⊤) := by
820	    have e_top : Submodule.map (e : M →ₗ[R] M') (⊤ : Submodule R M) = ⊤ := by
821	      rw [Submodule.map_top]; exact LinearEquiv.range e
822	    have e_symm_top :
823	        Submodule.map (e.symm : M' →ₗ[R] M) (⊤ : Submodule R M') = ⊤ := by
824	      rw [Submodule.map_top]; exact LinearEquiv.range e.symm
825	    refine ⟨?_, ?_⟩
826	    · intro h
827	      have hmap :=
828	        Submodule.map_smul'' I (⊤ : Submodule R M) (e : M →ₗ[R] M')
829	      rw [h, e_top] at hmap
830	      exact hmap.symm
831	    · intro h
832	      have hmap :=
833	        Submodule.map_smul'' I (⊤ : Submodule R M') (e.symm : M' →ₗ[R] M)
834	      rw [h, e_symm_top] at hmap
835	      exact hmap.symm
836	  -- Step 2: the `sSup` sets agree via `LinearEquiv.isRegular_congr`.
837	  unfold depth
838	  by_cases h : I • (⊤ : Submodule R M) = ⊤
839	  · simp [if_pos h, if_pos (hcond.mp h)]
840	  · rw [if_neg h, if_neg (mt hcond.mpr h)]
841	    congr 1
842	    ext n
843	    refine ⟨?_, ?_⟩
844	    · rintro ⟨rs, hlen, hmem, hreg⟩
845	      exact ⟨rs, hlen, hmem, (LinearEquiv.isRegular_congr e rs).mp hreg⟩
846	    · rintro ⟨rs, hlen, hmem, hreg⟩
847	      exact ⟨rs, hlen, hmem, (LinearEquiv.isRegular_congr e rs).mpr hreg⟩
848	
849	/-! ### Helper iter-194 Lane G (axiom-clean): depth of constant Pi equals depth of fiber.
850	
851	For a commutative ring `R`, ideal `I`, module `M`, and nonempty finite type `ι`,
852	`depth I (ι → M) = depth I M`. The proof goes through the regular-sequence
853	characterization: each `r`-action on `ι → M` is pointwise (so an `r ∈ R` is
854	regular on `ι → M` iff regular on `M`), and the quotient `(ι → M)/r·⊤`
855	identifies with `ι → M/r·⊤` via `Submodule.quotientPi`. The side condition
856	`I • ⊤ = ⊤` agrees on both sides via a `Pi.single` lifting argument.
857	
858	This is the substrate needed to close the `pd_R(M) = 0` case of the
859	Auslander–Buchsbaum formula (where `M ≃ₗ[R] Fin k → R` via a basis). -/
860	
861	/-- For any commutative ring `R`, ideal `I`, finite index `ι`, and module `M`,
862	the ideal-action `I • ⊤_{ι → M}` equals the pi-submodule of fibre `I • ⊤_M`s. -/
863	private lemma ideal_smul_top_pi_const
864	    {R : Type u} [CommRing R] {ι : Type*} [Fintype ι] [DecidableEq ι]
865	    (I : Ideal R) {M : Type v} [AddCommGroup M] [Module R M] :
866	    (I • (⊤ : Submodule R (ι → M))) =
867	      Submodule.pi (Set.univ : Set ι) (fun (_ : ι) => I • (⊤ : Submodule R M)) := by
868	  apply le_antisymm
869	  · intro f hf i _
870	    refine Submodule.smul_induction_on hf ?_ ?_
871	    · intro a hain x _
872	      change a • x i ∈ I • (⊤ : Submodule R M)
873	      exact Submodule.smul_mem_smul hain trivial
874	    · intro x y hx hy
875	      change (x + y) i ∈ _
876	      exact Submodule.add_mem _ hx hy
877	  · intro f hf
878	    rw [show f = ∑ j, Pi.single j (f j) from (Finset.univ_sum_single f).symm]
879	    refine Submodule.sum_mem _ ?_
880	    intro j _
881	    have hfj : f j ∈ I • (⊤ : Submodule R M) := hf j (Set.mem_univ j)
882	    have hmap :
883	        Pi.single j (f j) ∈
884	          Submodule.map (LinearMap.single R (fun (_ : ι) => M) j)
885	            (I • (⊤ : Submodule R M)) :=
886	      Submodule.mem_map.mpr ⟨f j, hfj, rfl⟩
887	    rw [Submodule.map_smul''] at hmap
888	    exact Submodule.smul_mono le_rfl le_top hmap
889	
890	/-- The side condition `I • ⊤ = ⊤` agrees on `ι → M` and `M` for nonempty
891	finite `ι`: a free product of fibre `I•⊤_M`-witnesses combines to a
892	`I•⊤_{ι → M}`-witness (via `Pi.single`-lifting), and conversely a
893	`Pi.single j m`-projection at `j` reads off the witness on the fibre. -/
894	private lemma ideal_smul_top_pi_const_eq_top_iff
895	    {R : Type u} [CommRing R] {ι : Type*} [Fintype ι] [DecidableEq ι] [Nonempty ι]
896	    (I : Ideal R) {M : Type v} [AddCommGroup M] [Module R M] :
897	    I • (⊤ : Submodule R (ι → M)) = ⊤ ↔ I • (⊤ : Submodule R M) = ⊤ := by
898	  constructor
899	  · intro h
900	    rw [eq_top_iff]
901	    intro m _
902	    obtain ⟨j⟩ := ‹Nonempty ι›
903	    have hsingle_mem :
904	        (Pi.single j m : ι → M) ∈ I • (⊤ : Submodule R (ι → M)) := by
905	      rw [h]; trivial
906	    rw [ideal_smul_top_pi_const] at hsingle_mem
907	    have := hsingle_mem j (Set.mem_univ j)
908	    rwa [Pi.single_eq_same] at this
909	  · intro h
910	    rw [ideal_smul_top_pi_const, eq_top_iff]
911	    intro f _ i _
912	    rw [h]
913	    trivial
914	
915	/-- `QuotSMulTop r (ι → M) ≃ₗ[R] ι → QuotSMulTop r M` for finite `ι`,
916	obtained by rewriting `r • ⊤ = Ideal.span {r} • ⊤` and using
917	`Submodule.quotientPi`. -/
918	private noncomputable def quotSMulTopPiConstLinearEquiv
919	    {R : Type u} [CommRing R] {ι : Type*} [Fintype ι] [DecidableEq ι] (r : R)
920	    {M : Type v} [AddCommGroup M] [Module R M] :
921	    QuotSMulTop r (ι → M) ≃ₗ[R] (ι → QuotSMulTop r M) := by
922	  refine (Submodule.quotEquivOfEq _ _ ?_).trans (Submodule.quotientPi _)
923	  rw [← Submodule.ideal_span_singleton_smul r (⊤ : Submodule R (ι → M))]
924	  rw [ideal_smul_top_pi_const]
925	  congr 1
926	  funext _
927	  exact Submodule.ideal_span_singleton_smul r ⊤
928	
929	/-- For nonempty finite `ι`, a list `rs : List R` is `(ι → M)`-regular iff it
930	is `M`-regular. Proof by induction on `rs`: the empty case reduces to
931	`Nontrivial (ι → M) ↔ Nontrivial M`; the cons case uses `Pi.isSMulRegular_iff`
932	(for the SMul-regular conjunct) plus `quotSMulTopPiConstLinearEquiv` +
933	`LinearEquiv.isRegular_congr` (to bridge the quotient regularity to the IH on
934	`QuotSMulTop r M`). -/
935	private lemma isRegular_pi_const_iff_of_nonempty
936	    {R : Type u} [CommRing R] {ι : Type*} [Fintype ι] [DecidableEq ι] [Nonempty ι]
937	    (rs : List R) :
938	    ∀ {M : Type v} [AddCommGroup M] [Module R M],
939	      RingTheory.Sequence.IsRegular (ι → M) rs ↔
940	        RingTheory.Sequence.IsRegular M rs := by
941	  induction rs with
942	  | nil =>
943	    intro M _ _
944	    refine ⟨?_, ?_⟩
945	    · rintro ⟨_, hPi_top⟩
946	      refine ⟨.nil R M, ?_⟩
947	      rw [Ideal.ofList_nil, Submodule.bot_smul] at hPi_top ⊢
948	      intro habs
949	      apply hPi_top
950	      rw [Submodule.eq_bot_iff] at habs ⊢
951	      intro f _
952	      funext i
953	      exact habs (f i) trivial
954	    · rintro ⟨_, hM_top⟩
955	      refine ⟨.nil R (ι → M), ?_⟩
956	      rw [Ideal.ofList_nil, Submodule.bot_smul] at hM_top ⊢
957	      obtain ⟨j⟩ := ‹Nonempty ι›
958	      intro habs
959	      apply hM_top
960	      rw [Submodule.eq_bot_iff] at habs ⊢
961	      intro m _
962	      have hsingle : (Pi.single j m : ι → M) = 0 := habs _ trivial
963	      have heval := congr_fun hsingle j
964	      rwa [Pi.single_eq_same, Pi.zero_apply] at heval
965	  | cons r rs' ih =>
966	    intro M _ _
967	    rw [RingTheory.Sequence.isRegular_cons_iff, RingTheory.Sequence.isRegular_cons_iff]
968	    refine and_congr ?_ ?_
969	    · constructor
970	      · intro h
971	        obtain ⟨j⟩ := ‹Nonempty ι›
972	        exact Pi.isSMulRegular_iff.mp h j
973	      · intro h
974	        exact Pi.isSMulRegular_iff.mpr fun _ => h
975	    · rw [LinearEquiv.isRegular_congr
976	        (quotSMulTopPiConstLinearEquiv (R := R) (ι := ι) r (M := M)) rs']
977	      exact ih (M := QuotSMulTop r M)
978	
979	/-- **Main iter-194 Lane G closure.** For any commutative ring `R`, ideal `I`,
980	`R`-module `M`, and nonempty finite type `ι`, the depth of the Pi module
981	`ι → M` equals the depth of `M`:
982	```
983	  depth I (ι → M) = depth I M.
984	```
985	This is the substrate for the `pd_R(M) = 0` case of the Auslander–Buchsbaum
986	formula: a finite free module `M ≃ₗ[R] Fin k → R` has `depth(M) = depth(R)`,
987	so `0 + depth(M) = depth(R)` holds. -/
988	lemma depth_pi_const_eq_depth_of_nonempty
989	    {R : Type u} [CommRing R] (I : Ideal R)
990	    {ι : Type*} [Fintype ι] [DecidableEq ι] [Nonempty ι]
991	    {M : Type v} [AddCommGroup M] [Module R M] :
992	    depth I (ι → M) = depth I M := by
993	  unfold depth
994	  by_cases h : I • (⊤ : Submodule R (ι → M)) = ⊤
995	  · rw [if_pos h, if_pos ((ideal_smul_top_pi_const_eq_top_iff I).mp h)]
996	  · rw [if_neg h, if_neg (mt (ideal_smul_top_pi_const_eq_top_iff I).mpr h)]
997	    congr 1
998	    ext n
999	    refine ⟨?_, ?_⟩
1000	    · rintro ⟨rs, hlen, hmem, hreg⟩
1001	      exact ⟨rs, hlen, hmem, (isRegular_pi_const_iff_of_nonempty rs).mp hreg⟩
1002	    · rintro ⟨rs, hlen, hmem, hreg⟩
1003	      exact ⟨rs, hlen, hmem, (isRegular_pi_const_iff_of_nonempty rs).mpr hreg⟩

1005	/-! ### Helper iter-198 Lane AB (axiom-clean): depth drops by exactly one for a regular
1006	element (Stacks `lemma-depth-drops-by-one`).
1007	
1008	For a Noetherian local ring `(R, 𝔪)`, a nonzero finite `R`-module `M`, and `x ∈ 𝔪` an
1009	`M`-regular element, the depth of `M/xM` is exactly one less than the depth of `M`:
1010	`depth(M/xM) + 1 = depth(M)`.
1011	
1012	This is one of the four substrate gaps for the Auslander–Buchsbaum n=k+1 step (the
1013	"depth-drops-by-one" piece). The proof packages the long-exact sequence of
1014	`Ext^*(κ, -)` on the SES `0 → M →[x] M → M/xM → 0`, using that
1015	`[x]` is zero on every `Ext^*(κ, M)` (since `x ∈ Ann κ`) to break the LES into short
1016	exact pieces `0 → Ext^i(κ, M) → Ext^i(κ, M/xM) → Ext^{i+1}(κ, M) → 0`.
1017	
1018	Routed through `depth_eq_smallest_ext_index` on both sides + the `ENat.forall_natCast_le_iff_le`
1019	two-direction bridge that converts a natural-number indexed predicate to a `ℕ∞` inequality. -/
1020	lemma depth_quotSMulTop_succ_eq_depth_of_isSMulRegular
1021	    {R : Type u} [CommRing R] [IsLocalRing R] [IsNoetherianRing R]
1022	    {M : Type u} [AddCommGroup M] [Module R M] [_root_.Module.Finite R M]
1023	    [Nontrivial M] {x : R} (hxMem : x ∈ IsLocalRing.maximalIdeal R)
1024	    (hxReg : IsSMulRegular M x) :
1025	    depth (IsLocalRing.maximalIdeal R) (QuotSMulTop x M) + 1 =
1026	      depth (IsLocalRing.maximalIdeal R) M := by
1027	  haveI hMxM_nt : Nontrivial (QuotSMulTop x M) :=
1028	    nontrivial_quotSMulTop_of_mem_maximalIdeal M hxMem
1029	  haveI : _root_.Module.Finite R (QuotSMulTop x M) := inferInstance
1030	  -- κ as a ModuleCat for compact notation.
1031	  set κ : ModuleCat.{u} R := ModuleCat.of R (IsLocalRing.ResidueField R) with hκ
1032	  -- x annihilates κ.
1033	  have hxAnnih : x ∈ _root_.Module.annihilator R (IsLocalRing.ResidueField R) := by
1034	    have h : _root_.Module.annihilator R (IsLocalRing.ResidueField R)
1035	        = IsLocalRing.maximalIdeal R := Ideal.annihilator_quotient
1036	    rw [h]; exact hxMem
1037	  -- The SES `0 → M →[x] M → M/xM → 0`.
1038	  let S : ShortComplex (ModuleCat.{u} R) :=
1039	    ModuleCat.smulShortComplex (ModuleCat.of R M) x
1040	  have hS : S.ShortExact := hxReg.smulShortComplex_shortExact
1041	  have hSf_eq_smul : S.f = x • (𝟙 (ModuleCat.of R M) : _ ⟶ _) := rfl
1042	  -- `[x]_*` is zero on every `Ext^i(κ, M)` via the helper `ext_smul_eq_zero_of_mem_annihilator`.
1043	  have hSf_kill : ∀ {i : ℕ} (e : Abelian.Ext.{u} κ (ModuleCat.of R M) i),
1044	      e.comp (CategoryTheory.Abelian.Ext.mk₀ S.f) (add_zero i) = 0 := by
1045	    intro i e
1046	    have hcomp :
1047	        e.comp (CategoryTheory.Abelian.Ext.mk₀ S.f) (add_zero i) = x • e := by
1048	      rw [hSf_eq_smul]
1049	      exact (CategoryTheory.Abelian.Ext.smul_eq_comp_mk₀ e x).symm
1050	    rw [hcomp]
1051	    exact ext_smul_eq_zero_of_mem_annihilator e hxAnnih
1052	  refine le_antisymm ?_ ?_
1053	  · -- (≥): depth(M/xM) + 1 ≤ depth M.
1054	    rw [← ENat.forall_natCast_le_iff_le]
1055	    intro a ha
1056	    rcases Nat.eq_zero_or_pos a with h0 | hpos
1057	    · subst h0; exact bot_le
1058	    · obtain ⟨n, rfl⟩ := Nat.exists_eq_succ_of_ne_zero (Nat.pos_iff_ne_zero.mp hpos)
1059	      have hcast : ((n + 1 : ℕ) : ℕ∞) = (n : ℕ∞) + 1 := by push_cast; ring
1060	      have hcast' : (Nat.succ n : ℕ) = n + 1 := rfl
1061	      rw [hcast'] at ha
1062	      rw [hcast] at ha
1063	      have hone_ne_top : (1 : ℕ∞) ≠ ⊤ := by simp
1064	      have hn_le_MxM :
1065	          (n : ℕ∞) ≤ depth (IsLocalRing.maximalIdeal R) (QuotSMulTop x M) :=
1066	        (ENat.add_le_add_iff_right hone_ne_top).mp ha
1067	      have hMxM_vanish : ∀ j : ℕ, j < n → ∀ e : Abelian.Ext.{u} κ
1068	            (ModuleCat.of R (QuotSMulTop x M)) j, e = 0 :=
1069	        (depth_eq_smallest_ext_index (M := QuotSMulTop x M) n).mp hn_le_MxM
1070	      change ((n.succ : ℕ) : ℕ∞) ≤ depth (IsLocalRing.maximalIdeal R) M
1071	      rw [hcast']
1072	      rw [depth_eq_smallest_ext_index]
1073	      intro i hi e
1074	      rcases Nat.eq_zero_or_pos i with hi0 | hi_pos
1075	      · subst hi0
1076	        -- i = 0 case: x being M-regular ⟹ S.f mono ⟹ postcomp_mk₀_injective_of_mono.
1077	        haveI hmono : CategoryTheory.Mono S.f := by
1078	          rw [ModuleCat.mono_iff_injective]
1079	          exact hxReg
1080	        have hinj := CategoryTheory.Abelian.Ext.postcomp_mk₀_injective_of_mono κ S.f
1081	        apply hinj
1082	        change e.comp (CategoryTheory.Abelian.Ext.mk₀ S.f) (add_zero 0)
1083	          = (0 : Abelian.Ext.{u} κ (ModuleCat.of R M) 0).comp
1084	              (CategoryTheory.Abelian.Ext.mk₀ S.f) (add_zero 0)
1085	        rw [CategoryTheory.Abelian.Ext.zero_comp]
1086	        exact hSf_kill e
1087	      · -- i ≥ 1: write i = j + 1, use `covariant_sequence_exact₁`.
1088	        obtain ⟨j, rfl⟩ := Nat.exists_eq_succ_of_ne_zero
1089	          (Nat.pos_iff_ne_zero.mp hi_pos)
1090	        have hj : j < n := by omega
1091	        obtain ⟨x₃, hx₃⟩ :=
1092	          CategoryTheory.Abelian.Ext.covariant_sequence_exact₁ κ hS e (hSf_kill e) rfl
1093	        have hx₃_zero : x₃ = 0 := hMxM_vanish j hj _
1094	        rw [hx₃_zero] at hx₃
1095	        simpa using hx₃.symm
1096	  · -- (≤): depth M ≤ depth(M/xM) + 1.
1097	    rw [← ENat.forall_natCast_le_iff_le]
1098	    intro a ha
1099	    rcases Nat.eq_zero_or_pos a with h0 | hpos
1100	    · subst h0; exact bot_le
1101	    · obtain ⟨m, rfl⟩ := Nat.exists_eq_succ_of_ne_zero (Nat.pos_iff_ne_zero.mp hpos)
1102	      have hcast' : (Nat.succ m : ℕ) = m + 1 := rfl
1103	      rw [hcast'] at ha
1104	      have hM_vanish : ∀ i : ℕ, i < m + 1 → ∀ e : Abelian.Ext.{u} κ
1105	            (ModuleCat.of R M) i, e = 0 :=
1106	        (depth_eq_smallest_ext_index (M := M) (m + 1)).mp ha
1107	      have hcast : ((m + 1 : ℕ) : ℕ∞) = (m : ℕ∞) + 1 := by push_cast; ring
1108	      change ((m.succ : ℕ) : ℕ∞) ≤ depth (IsLocalRing.maximalIdeal R) (QuotSMulTop x M) + 1
1109	      rw [hcast', hcast]
1110	      -- It suffices to show m ≤ depth(M/xM).
1111	      have hMxM_bound :
1112	          (m : ℕ∞) ≤ depth (IsLocalRing.maximalIdeal R) (QuotSMulTop x M) := by
1113	        rw [depth_eq_smallest_ext_index]
1114	        intro j hj e
1115	        -- Use `covariant_sequence_exact₃` at n₀ = j, n₁ = j + 1.
1116	        have he_extClass : e.comp hS.extClass (rfl : j + 1 = j + 1) = 0 :=
1117	          hM_vanish (j + 1) (by omega) _
1118	        obtain ⟨x₂, hx₂⟩ :=
1119	          CategoryTheory.Abelian.Ext.covariant_sequence_exact₃ κ hS e rfl he_extClass
1120	        have hx₂_zero : x₂ = 0 := hM_vanish j (by omega) _
1121	        rw [hx₂_zero] at hx₂
1122	        simpa using hx₂.symm

1125	/-! ### Helper iter-198 Lane AB (axiom-clean): regular-element existence from positive depth.
1126	
1127	For a Noetherian local ring `(R, 𝔪)` and a nonzero finite `R`-module `M`, the assertion
1128	`1 ≤ depth_𝔪(M)` is equivalent to the existence of an `M`-regular element `x ∈ 𝔪`. This
1129	is a direct consequence of unfolding the depth definition to a regular-sequence supremum
1130	(Nakayama rules out the `𝔪 • ⊤ = ⊤` trivialisation in the `Nontrivial M` setting) and
1131	reading off a length-1 witness.
1132	
1133	This is the substrate companion to `depth_quotSMulTop_succ_eq_depth_of_isSMulRegular`: the
1134	two together implement the Stacks tag 00LE step "depth-drops-by-one for any regular
1135	element" plus its prerequisite "the regular element exists". -/
1136	lemma exists_isSMulRegular_of_one_le_depth
1137	    {R : Type u} [CommRing R] [IsLocalRing R] [IsNoetherianRing R]
1138	    {M : Type u} [AddCommGroup M] [Module R M] [_root_.Module.Finite R M]
1139	    [Nontrivial M]
1140	    (h : (1 : ℕ∞) ≤ depth (IsLocalRing.maximalIdeal R) M) :
1141	    ∃ x : R, x ∈ IsLocalRing.maximalIdeal R ∧ IsSMulRegular M x := by
1142	  -- Unfold depth: `𝔪 • ⊤ ≠ ⊤` by Nakayama in the `Nontrivial M` finite setting.
1143	  have hne_M : (IsLocalRing.maximalIdeal R) • (⊤ : Submodule R M) ≠ ⊤ :=
1144	    Ne.symm (Submodule.top_ne_ideal_smul_of_le_jacobson_annihilator
1145	      (IsLocalRing.maximalIdeal_le_jacobson _))
1146	  have hdepth_unfold :
1147	      depth (IsLocalRing.maximalIdeal R) M
1148	        = sSup { k : ℕ∞ | ∃ rs : List R, (rs.length : ℕ∞) = k ∧
1149	          (∀ r ∈ rs, r ∈ IsLocalRing.maximalIdeal R)
1150	          ∧ RingTheory.Sequence.IsRegular M rs } := by
1151	    rw [depth, if_neg hne_M]
1152	  rw [hdepth_unfold] at h
1153	  have hlt : (0 : ℕ∞) < sSup { k : ℕ∞ | ∃ rs : List R, (rs.length : ℕ∞) = k ∧
1154	      (∀ r ∈ rs, r ∈ IsLocalRing.maximalIdeal R)
1155	      ∧ RingTheory.Sequence.IsRegular M rs } := by
1156	    calc (0 : ℕ∞) < 1 := by norm_num
1157	      _ ≤ _ := h
1158	  obtain ⟨k, ⟨rs, hrs_len, hrs_mem, hrs_reg⟩, hk_lt⟩ := lt_sSup_iff.mp hlt
1159	  -- rs has positive length (since (0 : ℕ∞) < ↑rs.length).
1160	  have hrs_pos : 0 < rs.length := by
1161	    by_contra h_neg
1162	    push Not at h_neg
1163	    have hk0 : rs.length = 0 := Nat.le_zero.mp h_neg
1164	    rw [hk0] at hrs_len
1165	    simp only [Nat.cast_zero] at hrs_len
1166	    rw [← hrs_len] at hk_lt
1167	    exact absurd hk_lt (lt_irrefl _)
1168	  rcases rs with _ | ⟨x, rs_tail⟩
1169	  · simp at hrs_pos
1170	  · refine ⟨x, hrs_mem x List.mem_cons_self, ?_⟩
1171	    exact ((RingTheory.Sequence.isRegular_cons_iff M x rs_tail).mp hrs_reg).1

1173	/-! ### Helper iter-199 Lane AB-gap1 (axiom-clean): minimal surjection substrate.
1174	
1175	For a finite `R`-module `M` over a local ring `R`, there exists a surjective
1176	`R`-linear map `f : (Fin n → R) →ₗ[R] M` of the **minimal possible rank**
1177	`n = dim_κ (κ ⊗_R M)` (where `κ = R/𝔪` is the residue field) whose **kernel
1178	is contained in `𝔪 • ⊤`**. This is the first step of constructing a *minimal
1179	finite free resolution*: iterating the construction on the kernel (which is
1180	itself finitely generated when `R` is Noetherian) produces successive
1181	syzygies whose differential maps each have image in `𝔪` times their target.
1182	
1183	This is the first substrate piece of gap (1) (Stacks
1184	`lemma-add-trivial-complex`) in the Auslander–Buchsbaum closure chain
1185	(`auslander_buchsbaum_formula_succ_pd`). It packages the basic
1186	**Nakayama-lift** of a κ-basis of `κ ⊗_R M` to an `R`-spanning family in `M`
1187	and reads off the kernel-containment from linear independence of the basis
1188	combined with the `1 ⊗_R -` evaluation.
1189	
1190	Mathlib substrate used (all axiom-clean):
1191	* `IsLocalRing.span_eq_top_of_tmul_eq_basis` — Nakayama lift of a κ-basis.
1192	* `TensorProduct.mk_surjective` — the `1 ⊗_R -` map is surjective for the
1193	  residue-field tensor.
1194	* `Module.Basis.constr_range` — range of the linear extension equals span of
1195	  the chosen image set.
1196	* `Module.Basis.linearIndependent` — independence of a κ-basis.
1197	* `IsLocalRing.residue_eq_zero_iff` — `r ∈ 𝔪 ↔ residue r = 0`. -/
1198	lemma exists_minimalSurjection_finite_localRing
1199	    (R : Type u) [CommRing R] [IsLocalRing R]
1200	    (M : Type u) [AddCommGroup M] [Module R M] [_root_.Module.Finite R M] :
1201	    ∃ (n : ℕ) (f : (Fin n → R) →ₗ[R] M),
1202	      Function.Surjective f ∧
1203	      n = _root_.Module.finrank (IsLocalRing.ResidueField R)
1204	        (TensorProduct R (IsLocalRing.ResidueField R) M) ∧
1205	      LinearMap.ker f ≤ (IsLocalRing.maximalIdeal R) • ⊤ := by
1206	  set κ := IsLocalRing.ResidueField R with hκ
1207	  set n := _root_.Module.finrank κ (TensorProduct R κ M) with hn
1208	  -- Pick a κ-basis of `κ ⊗_R M`.
1209	  let b : _root_.Module.Basis (Fin n) κ (TensorProduct R κ M) :=
1210	    _root_.Module.finBasis κ (TensorProduct R κ M)
1211	  -- The canonical map `(1 : κ) ⊗_R -` is surjective.
1212	  have hsurj_mk : Function.Surjective ((TensorProduct.mk R κ M) 1) := by
1213	    apply TensorProduct.mk_surjective
1214	    exact Ideal.Quotient.mk_surjective
1215	  -- Lift each basis element to a representative in M.
1216	  choose lift hlift using hsurj_mk
1217	  let m : Fin n → M := fun i => lift (b i)
1218	  have hm : ∀ i, (1 : κ) ⊗ₜ[R] m i = b i := fun i => hlift (b i)
1219	  -- Define `f` by sending each standard basis vector of `Fin n → R` to `m i`.
1220	  let f : (Fin n → R) →ₗ[R] M := (Pi.basisFun R (Fin n)).constr R m
1221	  -- Evaluation: `f x = Σ x i • m i`.
1222	  have hf_eval : ∀ x : Fin n → R, f x = ∑ i, x i • m i := by
1223	    intro x
1224	    rw [show f x = ((Pi.basisFun R (Fin n)).constr R) m x from rfl,
1225	        _root_.Module.Basis.constr_apply]
1226	    have h : (Pi.basisFun R (Fin n)).repr x = Finsupp.equivFunOnFinite.symm x := by
1227	      ext i; rw [Pi.basisFun_repr]; rfl
1228	    rw [h, Finsupp.sum_fintype _ _ (by intros; simp)]
1229	    exact Finset.sum_congr rfl (fun i _ => by simp)
1230	  -- Range = span of `m`.
1231	  have hf_range : LinearMap.range f = Submodule.span R (Set.range m) :=
1232	    _root_.Module.Basis.constr_range _ _
1233	  -- Nakayama: span of `m i` equals all of `M`.
1234	  have hspan : Submodule.span R (Set.range m) = ⊤ :=
1235	    IsLocalRing.span_eq_top_of_tmul_eq_basis m b hm
1236	  refine ⟨n, f, ?_, rfl, ?_⟩
1237	  · exact LinearMap.range_eq_top.mp (by rw [hf_range, hspan])
1238	  · -- Kernel containment in `𝔪 • ⊤`.
1239	    intro x hx
1240	    have hfx : f x = 0 := hx
1241	    rw [hf_eval] at hfx
1242	    -- Apply `(1 : κ) ⊗_R -` to `Σ x i • m i = 0`.
1243	    have h1 : (1 : κ) ⊗ₜ[R] (∑ i, x i • m i) = (0 : TensorProduct R κ M) := by
1244	      rw [hfx]; exact TensorProduct.tmul_zero _ _
1245	    rw [TensorProduct.tmul_sum] at h1
1246	    -- Rewrite each summand: `1 ⊗_R (x i • m i) = residue(x i) • b i`.
1247	    have hrewrite : ∀ i, (1 : κ) ⊗ₜ[R] (x i • m i)
1248	        = (IsLocalRing.residue R (x i) : κ) • b i := by
1249	      intro i
1250	      rw [show ((1 : κ) ⊗ₜ[R] (x i • m i))
1251	          = x i • ((1 : κ) ⊗ₜ[R] m i) from
1252	        (TensorProduct.tmul_smul (R := R) (x i) (1 : κ) (m i))]
1253	      rw [hm i]; rfl
1254	    rw [show (∑ i, (1 : κ) ⊗ₜ[R] (x i • m i))
1255	        = ∑ i, (IsLocalRing.residue R (x i) : κ) • b i from
1256	      Finset.sum_congr rfl (fun i _ => hrewrite i)] at h1
1257	    -- Linear independence of `b` forces each `residue (x i) = 0`.
1258	    have hlin : LinearIndependent κ b := b.linearIndependent
1259	    have hall : ∀ i, (IsLocalRing.residue R (x i) : κ) = 0 := by
1260	      have := Fintype.linearIndependent_iff.mp hlin
1261	        (fun i => IsLocalRing.residue R (x i)) h1
1262	      exact fun i => this i
1263	    -- Convert each component-in-𝔪 to `x ∈ 𝔪 • ⊤` via `Pi.single` decomposition.
1264	    have hx_pi : ∀ i, x i ∈ IsLocalRing.maximalIdeal R := by
1265	      intro i
1266	      have : IsLocalRing.residue R (x i) = 0 := hall i
1267	      rwa [IsLocalRing.residue_eq_zero_iff] at this
1268	    rw [show x = ∑ i, Pi.single i (x i) from (Finset.univ_sum_single x).symm]
1269	    refine Submodule.sum_mem _ ?_
1270	    intro i _
1271	    have hsingle :
1272	        (Pi.single i (x i) : Fin n → R)
1273	          = (x i) • (Pi.single i (1 : R) : Fin n → R) := by
1274	      ext j; by_cases hij : i = j <;> simp [Pi.single, Function.update, hij]
1275	    rw [hsingle]
1276	    exact Submodule.smul_mem_smul (hx_pi i) trivial

1373	    [Nontrivial (LinearMap.ker f)] :
1374	    min (depth (IsLocalRing.maximalIdeal R) R)

1441	failure of vanishing at `↑(D+1)` must therefore occur at index `D`.) This is
1442	the input that exhibits a nonzero class in the base case of Auslander–Buchsbaum:

1445	    {R : Type u} [CommRing R] [IsLocalRing R] [IsNoetherianRing R]
1446	    {M : Type u} [AddCommGroup M] [Module R M] [_root_.Module.Finite R M]
1447	    [Nontrivial M] {D : ℕ}
1448	    (hD : depth (IsLocalRing.maximalIdeal R) M = (D : ℕ∞)) :
1449	    ∃ e : Abelian.Ext.{u} (ModuleCat.of R (IsLocalRing.ResidueField R))

1451	  -- Below `D`, all Ext vanish.
1452	  have hvanish : ∀ i : ℕ, i < D → ∀ e : Abelian.Ext.{u}
1453	      (ModuleCat.of R (IsLocalRing.ResidueField R)) (ModuleCat.of R M) i, e = 0 :=
1454	    (depth_eq_smallest_ext_index (M := M) D).mp (by rw [hD])

1468	/-! ### Helper iter-201 Lane AB Path B (axiom-clean): matrix decomposition and
1469	matrix-collapse on `Ext`.
1470	
1471	For an R-linear map `A : R^m →ₗ R^n` between standard free modules over a
1472	commutative ring R, A decomposes as `A = ∑_{(i,j)} A_{i,j} • E_{i,j}` where
1473	`A_{i,j} = (A (Pi.single j 1)) i` is the matrix entry and `E_{i,j}` is the
1474	"elementary" linear map sending `Pi.single j 1 ↦ Pi.single i 1`. From this
1475	decomposition combined with R-bilinearity of `Ext.comp` and the axiom-clean
1476	helper `ext_smul_eq_zero_of_mem_annihilator`, we obtain the matrix-collapse
1477	result: if every entry of A lies in `Ann_R N`, then the induced postcomposition
1478	`Ext^p(N, R^m) → Ext^p(N, R^n)` via `mk₀ (ofHom A)` is the zero map.
1479	
1480	This is the key new substrate for closing the **Path B base case `pd M = 1`**
1481	of the Auslander–Buchsbaum formula: given a minimal surjection `f : R^n ↠ M`
1482	with `ker f` free of positive rank, the inclusion `ker f ≅ R^k ↪ R^n` is an
1483	R-linear map with entries in 𝔪 (by minimality `ker f ≤ 𝔪 • ⊤`); the
1484	matrix-collapse then forces the LES injectivity needed to conclude
1485	`depth M < depth R`. -/
1486

1592	/-! ## §5. The Auslander–Buchsbaum formula
1593	
1594	For a nonzero finite module `M` of finite projective dimension over a
1595	Noetherian local ring `(R, 𝔪)`, the **Auslander–Buchsbaum formula** reads
1596	```
1597	  pd_R(M) + depth(M) = depth(R)
1598	```
1599	(Stacks tag 090V). The proof inducts on `depth(M)`: the base case
1600	`depth(M) = 0` uses a minimal finite free resolution of `M` and the
1601	"what is exact" criterion (Stacks 00MF) plus iterated application of the
1602	depth-on-a-short-exact-sequence lemma (§4) to bound `depth(R)` against the
1603	resolution length; the inductive step picks a common non-zero-divisor
1604	`x ∈ 𝔪` on both `R` and `M`, applies the snake lemma to obtain a minimal
1605	finite free resolution of `M/xM` over `R/xR` of the same length, and uses
1606	the inductive hypothesis on `M/xM` over `R/xR`.
1607	
1608	Blueprint reference: `thm:auslander_buchsbaum` (Stacks tag 090V). -/

1614	`j + depth(K) = depth(R)` together with the two `depth_of_short_exact`
1615	inequalities (parts (2) and (3)) on the SES `0 → K → R^n → M → 0` — after
1616	identifying `depth(R^n) = depth(R)` — into the conclusion
1617	`(j+1) + depth(M) = depth(R)`. Valid for `j ≥ 1` (the inductive step
1618	`pd M ≥ 2`); the `j = 0` / `pd M = 1` base case is handled separately via
1619	matrix-collapse since part (3) is then vacuous. -/

1621	    (hIH : (j : ℕ∞) + dK = d)

1623	    (h3 : min d (dM + 1) ≤ dK)
1624	    (hj : 1 ≤ j) :

1626	  subst hIH
1627	  cases dK with
1628	  | top =>
1629	    -- `dK = ⊤`: part (2) forces `dM = ⊤`, both sides are `⊤`.

1631	      have : (⊤ : ℕ∞) ≤ dM := by simpa using h2
1632	      simpa using top_le_iff.mp this
1633	    subst hdM; simp
1634	  | coe K =>
1635	    cases dM with
1636	    | top =>
1637	      -- `dM = ⊤`: part (3) gives `↑(j+K) ≤ ↑K`, i.e. `j ≤ 0`, contradiction.
1638	      exfalso
1639	      have : (j : ℕ∞) + (K : ℕ∞) ≤ (K : ℕ∞) := by simpa using h3
1640	      rw [← Nat.cast_add, Nat.cast_le] at this
1641	      omega
1642	    | coe m =>

1647	            cases K with

1649	            | succ K => push_cast; rfl,
1650	          ← Nat.cast_add] at h2'

1652	          ← Nat.cast_add] at h3'
1653	      simp only [min_le_iff, Nat.cast_le] at h2' h3'

2540	Stacks 00NQ proof sketch (induction on `dim R = spanFinrank 𝔪`):
2541	* Base `dim R = 0`: then `𝔪 = ⊥`, hence `R` is a field, hence a domain.
2542	* Step `dim R = d + 1 ≥ 1`:
2543	  - Pick `x ∈ 𝔪 \ 𝔪²` (axiom-clean via Nakayama; see
2544	    `exists_notMemSq_of_spanFinrank_pos` below).
2545	  - By the cotangent dim-drop (`finrank_cotangentSpace_quot_span_singleton_succ`,
2546	    iter-188 Lane G1 closure) **plus** Krull's principal ideal theorem
2547	    (`ringKrullDim_quotient_span_singleton_succ_eq_ringKrullDim_of_mem_jacobson`
2548	    gives the `≥` half), `R / (x)` is regular local of dim `d`.
2549	  - By induction, `R / (x)` is a domain, hence `(x)` is a prime ideal of `R`.
2550	  - Let `𝔭` be a minimal prime of `R` with `𝔭 ⊆ (x)`. For `y ∈ 𝔭`, write
2551	    `y = xz`; since `𝔭` is prime and `x ∉ 𝔭` (else `x ∈ 𝔭 ⊆ (x)` doesn't
2552	    immediately give a contradiction but combined with `dim R/(x) = d` and
2553	    `x ∈ 𝔭` height-zero we get `𝔭 = 0` which is the conclusion), we get
2554	    `z ∈ 𝔭`. Hence `𝔭 ⊆ x 𝔭`, so iterating, `𝔭 ⊆ ∩ x^n 𝔭 ⊆ ∩ 𝔪^n = 0`
2555	    (Krull intersection). So `𝔭 = 0`, and `R` is a domain.
2556	
2557	Full formalization is ~300 LOC (involves Krull's PIT, prime avoidance,
2558	Krull intersection, minimal-primes-are-finite); deferred to a future iter

3210	`exists_isSMulRegular_quotient_isRegularLocal_succ`. Assembly path:
3211	
3212	1. Helper extracts `x ∈ 𝔪` with `IsSMulRegular R x` AND `IsRegularLocalRing
3213	   (R⧸(x))` of `spanFinrank = k`.

3215	   `rs'_q : List (R ⧸ (x))` of length `k` in the maximal ideal there.

3217	   `Ideal.Quotient.mk_surjective`; the section property gives
3218	   `rs.map (Ideal.Quotient.mk _) = rs'_q`.

3227	private lemma regularLocal_inductive_step {R : Type u} [CommRing R] [IsLocalRing R]
3228	    [IsNoetherianRing R] [IsRegularLocalRing R] {k : ℕ}
3229	    (hdim : (IsLocalRing.maximalIdeal R).spanFinrank = k + 1)
3230	    (IH : ∀ (R' : Type u) [CommRing R'] [IsLocalRing R'] [IsNoetherianRing R']
3231	            [IsRegularLocalRing R'],
3232	            (IsLocalRing.maximalIdeal R').spanFinrank = k →
3233	            ∃ rs : List R', rs.length = k ∧
3234	              (∀ r ∈ rs, r ∈ IsLocalRing.maximalIdeal R') ∧
3235	              RingTheory.Sequence.IsRegular R' rs) :
3236	    ∃ rs : List R, rs.length = k + 1 ∧
3237	      (∀ r ∈ rs, r ∈ IsLocalRing.maximalIdeal R) ∧
3238	      RingTheory.Sequence.IsRegular R rs := by
3239	  -- Step 1: extract `x ∈ 𝔪` regular on `R` with `R ⧸ (x)` regular local of
3240	  -- `spanFinrank = k`.  This is the consolidated Stacks 00NQ + 00NU substrate.
3241	  obtain ⟨x, hxMem, hxReg, hRLR, hdim_quot⟩ :=
3242	    exists_isSMulRegular_quotient_isRegularLocal_succ hdim
3243	  -- Step 2: apply IH on `R ⧸ (x)` — this gives a regular sequence of length
3244	  -- `k` on the quotient, valued in its maximal ideal.
3245	  obtain ⟨rs'_q, hlen_q, hmem_q, hreg_q⟩ := IH (R ⧸ Ideal.span {x}) hdim_quot
3246	  -- Step 3: lift `rs'_q : List (R ⧸ (x))` to `rs' : List R` via the right
3247	  -- inverse of the (surjective) quotient ring hom.
3248	  let mkq : R →+* R ⧸ Ideal.span {x} := Ideal.Quotient.mk _
3249	  let g : R ⧸ Ideal.span {x} → R := Function.surjInv Ideal.Quotient.mk_surjective
3250	  have hg : ∀ y, mkq (g y) = y := Function.surjInv_eq _
3251	  let rs' : List R := rs'_q.map g
3252	  have hlen_rs' : rs'.length = k := by simp [rs', hlen_q]
3253	  have hmkmap : rs'.map mkq = rs'_q := by
3254	    change (rs'_q.map g).map mkq = rs'_q
3255	    rw [List.map_map]
3256	    conv_rhs => rw [← List.map_id rs'_q]
3257	    exact List.map_congr_left fun y _ => hg y
3258	  -- Step 4: each element of `rs'` lies in `𝔪 R` via the comap of `𝔪 (R⧸(x))`.
3259	  -- The maximal ideal of `R ⧸ (x)` comaps back to `𝔪 R` (it's the *unique*
3260	  -- maximal ideal of `R` containing `Ideal.span {x} ⊆ 𝔪`).

2064	  -- for `M ≃ₗ[R] (Fin k → R)`; the residual `depth(R^k) = depth(R)` step is
2065	  -- the remaining substrate gap (a single direct-sum-of-modules depth fact).
2066	  rcases Nat.eq_zero_or_pos n with hn0 | hn_pos
2067	  · -- **Base case `n = 0`**: `pd_R(M) = 0` ⟹ `M` projective ⟹ (finite + local)
2068	    -- `M` free ⟹ `depth(M) = depth(R)`. The formula
2069	    -- `0 + depth(M) = depth(R)` collapses to `depth(M) = depth(R)`.
2070	    subst hn0
2071	    rw [Nat.cast_zero, zero_add]
2072	    -- Step 1: unfold `_root_.Module.projectiveDimension` to the categorical
2073	    -- form, then apply `projectiveDimension_eq_zero_iff`.
2074	    have hpd' :
2075	        CategoryTheory.projectiveDimension (ModuleCat.of R M) = (0 : WithBot ℕ∞) := by
2076	      unfold _root_.Module.projectiveDimension at _hpd
2077	      exact _hpd
2078	    obtain ⟨hproj, _hNonZero⟩ :=
2079	      (CategoryTheory.projectiveDimension_eq_zero_iff _).mp hpd'
2080	    -- Step 2: `CategoryTheory.Projective (ModuleCat.of R M)` ⟹ `Module.Projective R M`.
2081	    have hMproj : _root_.Module.Projective R M :=
2082	      (IsProjective.iff_projective M).mpr hproj
2083	    -- Step 3: `Module.Projective` ⟹ `Module.Flat`.
2084	    haveI : _root_.Module.Flat R M := _root_.Module.Flat.of_projective
2085	    -- Step 4: `Module.Flat` + `IsLocalRing` + `Module.Finite` ⟹ `Module.Free`.
2086	    haveI : _root_.Module.Free R M := _root_.Module.free_of_flat_of_isLocalRing
2087	    -- Step 5: with `M` finite free + `Nontrivial`, identify
2088	    -- `depth(M) = depth(R)` via the `Module.finBasis` equivalence and the
2089	    -- axiom-clean `depth_eq_of_linearEquiv` helper. The residual is the
2090	    -- single, narrower statement: `depth(Fin k → R) = depth(R)` for `k ≥ 1`.
2091	    have hk : 0 < _root_.Module.finrank R M :=
2092	      (_root_.Module.finrank_pos_iff_of_free R M).mpr inferInstance
2093	    set k : ℕ := _root_.Module.finrank R M with hk_def
2094	    -- Build the equivalence `M ≃ₗ[R] (Fin k → R)` via the chosen basis.
2095	    let e : M ≃ₗ[R] (Fin k → R) := (_root_.Module.finBasis R M).equivFun

2098	        = Module.depth (IsLocalRing.maximalIdeal R) (Fin k → R) :=
